sections.paragraph:
ἐγκάθετος, ον (ἐν + κατὰ + ἵημι; ἔνκ-Tdf., W-H.—Hyperid., Fgm. 56; Demosth., Ep. 3, 34; Polyb. 3, 15, 1; Ps.-Pla., Axioch. 368e; Jos., Bell. 2, 27; 6, 286; Job 19:12; 31:9) pert. to having the task of obtaining information secretly, hired to lie in wait, subst. ἐγκάθετοι spies Lk 20:20.—S. DELG s.v. ἵημι.

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 272
